entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that tracks user, session, and autonomous agent states. It utilizes temporal reasoning and hybrid retrieval to ensure past and future events are ranked with time-aware precision.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated brain for Hermes Agent features a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts typed entity relationships. It enhances retrieval through backlink-boosted ranking and includes an automated overnight process for memory consolidation and citation self-correction.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a long-term learning loop through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It provides an enterprise-ready LLM wrapper that supports PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database for robust storage of agent experiences.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that uses asynchronous reasoning to extract conclusions from conversation history and events. It employs a peer-centric model to track the evolving relationships between users and agents, delivering prompt-ready context via dedicated SDKs.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a five-role architecture to manage strategy and curation, helping agents succeed on complex tasks. It focuses on knowledge persistence and iterative self-improvement, producing detailed execution traces and reusable playbooks.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM is a lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG structure to prevent message loss during compaction. It provides agents with specialized tools to search, describe, and expand compressed history stored in a persistent SQLite backend.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library, enabling private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent users. It includes an automated setup script for Docker and supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first memory engine that operates entirely without cloud dependencies or API keys. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval system combining BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs, integrating directly via MCP servers and agent plugins.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: ContextPilot optimizes long-context inference by reordering and deduplicating shared context blocks to reduce input tokens. It significantly boosts prefill throughput for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, achieving up to 3x faster processing speeds.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML format called engrams to facilitate zero-cost local storage. It applies ACT-R activation principles to strengthen relevant facts while allowing stale information to decay over time.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases, documents, and media with automatic summarization and OCR. It uses edge tags to create bidirectional relationships between notes, making them easily navigable for agents via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory system designed for sub-millisecond latency in read, write, and search operations. It offers a native Hermes Agent plugin that utilizes local SQLite storage with built-in vector and full-text search capabilities.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: Brainstack is a memory kernel that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval into a single stack. Its five-shelf architecture prevents flat memory blobs, providing a structured MemoryProvider plugin powered by SQLite and Kuzu.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to predict future needs. It is a local-first solution using SQLite-vec and GGUF models, offering an MCP-native server for seamless integration with coding agents.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: This utility focuses on reducing tool-schema overhead for Hermes Agent by using BM25 keyword ranking to select only relevant tools. It supports deferred tool loading and includes a dashboard to track token savings and schema efficiency.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: Provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables sub-second AI skill discovery by adhering to the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: An agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement based on interaction history. It unifies skill libraries across various agents and devices, with native support for Hermes and Claude Code.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: Audits and rewrites content to remove detectable AI patterns using a 109-entry tiered word replacement table. It employs two-pass audits to eliminate significance inflation and recycled transitions.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library offering modular frameworks for UX design, architecture, marketing, and product strategy. These skills are compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compliant agents.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: Brings type-safe agentskills.io support to Pydantic AI through filesystem-based or programmatic Python definitions. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: Translates natural language descriptions into .drawio XML diagrams with exports available in PNG, SVG, PDF, and JPG. It features six presets for UML and ML models and a self-check loop to fix layout issues.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Teaches Hermes Agent to author its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problems. It identifies structural conservation laws in codebases and generates detailed constraint reports.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Enables interaction with the Chainlink oracle network, including support for CCIP, VRF, and Data Feeds. These skills follow the agentskills.io specification for seamless agent compatibility.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: Implements literate programming by weaving prose, diagrams, and source code into .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits back to the documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Facilitates cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories. It provides an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality training data to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry for reusable AI skills featuring an OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI for lifecycle management. It supports skill monetization through Stripe and the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official integration for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 image generation, providing standardized prompting guidelines. It supports JSON structured prompting, precise hex colors, and API patterns for webhooks and polling.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically propose and generate reusable skills. It produces both Python plugin scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents that replaces chat history with local-first, on-disk state management. It uses a structured spec-to-ship workflow with passive handoff envelopes for task resumption.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: Provides autonomous execution for complex, multi-step tasks with real-time progress tracking. It ensures reliability through built-in checkpoints and rollback procedures for safe recovery.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize context token usage. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon with hot-reload support.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Converts repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It ensures transparency with file-and-line level provenance audit trails.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and installing skills, featuring security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes a creator marketplace and an agent-to-agent trust scoring system.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: A C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with MCP integration. It leverages Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading and supports SKILL.md progressive disclosure.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: Allows Hermes Agent to manage Nextcloud files, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It ensures secure communication over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive collection of Nix packages for AI coding agents, including Hermes, with automated daily updates to ensure access to the latest versions. It is ideal for developers seeking a reproducible, Nix-based environment that includes orchestrators, CLI agents, and unified API proxies.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by utilizing NVIDIA OpenShell for hardware-level sandboxing and kernel-level enforcement. It offers multi-layer protection against unauthorized network egress and filesystem writes through pre-configured, strict security policy presets.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services with a focus on secure secrets handling via sops-nix. It supports a hybrid skill architecture, allowing for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI tool installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and supports local model execution via LM Studio. It features a GUI, a dynamic tool maker, and portable AI generation servers, eliminating the need for Docker or complex installation processes.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes mini-swe-agent and the official Hermes installation. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using flexible build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances with distinct identities and workspaces from a single web application. It is tailored for complex operations, featuring hierarchy-aware delegation and enterprise MCP access for external integrations.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for headless operation and the autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs via Nous Portal integration.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack integrated with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based agent interaction. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages complex multi-agent workflows using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It serves as a central hub for Hermes by integrating major frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen while enforcing human-in-the-loop oversight through its Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms that support over 23 LLM providers, including native Hermes and local Ollama integrations. It distinguishes itself with durable memory systems and a unique 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism that allows agents to acquire new capabilities through reviewed interactions.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: Focused on performance optimization, this project uses an automated outer-loop search process to refine the harness code used by Hermes Agent. It orchestrates benchmark evaluations across TBLite and TB2, utilizing structured candidate mutation to push the performance frontiers of the model.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: This competitive arena orchestrates two rival AI companies, each powered by nine autonomous agents, in a race to gain GitHub stars. By leveraging Hermes Agent for task execution and learning, it provides a real-time dashboard to visualize agent-vs-agent competition and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates a massive pipeline of 17 specialized agents covering everything from research to infrastructure. It ensures high-quality code output by enforcing mandatory reviewer and tester gates while utilizing MCP servers for external web fetching and documentation access.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase process that enforces architectural integrity through automated graph-diff gates at every stage of development.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation tool routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code using an ACP-style workflow. It is designed to prevent context pollution in parent agents by isolating delegation tasks and enforcing safety through strict timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI provides local data visualization for tracking token usage and costs across multiple AI coding environments. It integrates directly with Hermes Agent alongside platforms like Claude Code and Gemini to help developers monitor their resource consumption.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files for custom banners, spinners, and ASCII art. Themes can inherit from default settings, making it simple to create and share partial skin definitions within the community.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to the Claude Code Channel, allowing them to run natively without external processes or OAuth. It specifically includes specialized MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, this tool features native MCP integration for external harnesses like Hermes Agent. It provides a human-in-the-loop supervisory UI for live approvals and automatically infers page schemas to detect structured entities and forms.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific folders with local configurations and memories. It enables seamless switching between default and project-local agents, each with their own custom instructions and toolsets.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter uses regex and AST analysis to detect failure modes in agent configurations and system prompts without requiring LLM calls. It supports multiple file formats and provides HERM v1.1 scoring to identify ambiguity or missing constraints in tool descriptions.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills into the Hermes YAML format. It ensures a smooth transfer of complex model configurations and credentials from local environment files.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment stack uses Docker to automate the setup of Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, focused on a zero-cost, free-model workflow.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This interface provides a streamlined three-panel layout that mirrors the full feature set of the Hermes CLI for web and mobile users. It distinguishes itself with a visual context ring for real-time token monitoring and an integrated file browser for direct workspace interaction.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-channel connectivity, allowing users to configure and manage Hermes across platforms like Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It features robust session management and detailed usage analytics to track token consumption and estimated costs across different models.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace integrates a terminal, memory browser, and skill manager into a single interface designed for zero-fork compatibility with vanilla installations. It is unique for its Swarm Mode, which enables autonomous multi-agent orchestration and role-based dispatching.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway that acts as a command center for starting, stopping, and configuring individual agent profiles. The UI features collapsible tool-call cards and a suite of system metrics to monitor the health and performance of the underlying AI stack.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Built specifically for macOS, this desktop client establishes direct SSH connections to remote Hermes hosts without requiring intermediary APIs. It provides a native experience for managing remote files and sessions, supplemented by a Kanban board for task organization.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React application brings a terminal-style chat experience directly into the Telegram ecosystem as a Mini App. It supports background agent spawning and live output monitoring, complete with system health gauges and cron job management.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows solution that runs without admin rights or Docker, offering a GUI for local models via LM Studio. It stands out with over 100 built-in tools—including TTS and ComfyUI—and a dynamic tool maker for creating custom Python handlers.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This companion app simplifies the setup process with a guided installer for both local instances and remote API connections. It excels in integration, supporting 16 different messaging gateways and providing built-in management for 14 distinct toolsets and memory systems.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a read-only view of configuration files, environment variables, and cron jobs. It features FTS5-powered search for deep inspection of conversation histories and real-time tracking of gateway status and costs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: This self-hosted workspace emphasizes modularity through an integrated marketplace for discovering and installing skills and MCP servers. It utilizes isolated profiles to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate while providing real-time tool approval cards in the chat.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Featuring a modern glassmorphic design, this single-file React interface offers real-time log streaming and inline workspace previews. It provides a lightweight yet capable environment for managing MCP tools, skills, and scheduled agent tasks.
